info.title: MFMF Laboratory API
info.description: Computational laboratory for 'Mathematical Foundations of Modern Finance'. Every endpoint implements a Modern Finance Laboratory module of the book; every module carries the book's validation checks with seeded, reproducible runs.

post.description: E1 — from a single number to a valuation distribution.
E2 — sweep the traded price and watch the arbitrage region.
E3 — the physical measure P versus the risk-neutral measure Q.
E4 — the incomplete third-state market and its pricing bound.
E1 — two analysts (normal vs Student-t) and the tail crossing.
E2 — the unstable rolling-window degrees of freedom.
E3 — Gaussian vs t4 copula joint-tail counts.
E1 — the tower property verified numerically.
E2 — the clairvoyance detector rejects a look-ahead rule.
E3 — the law of total variance / smoothing.
E1 — the state-price segment and the stake's bounds.
E2 — complete the market with the recession claim.
E3 — price the stake three ways (psi, q, m).
E4 — the Hansen-Jagannathan wedge.
E1 — predictable rules average to zero; a peek buys a slope.
E2 — the density process; risk-neutral vs ZH pricing agree.
E3 — the (V, Delta) hedging lattice.
E1 — Donsker convergence; the collar tends to -2.76.
E2 — realized variance across sampling frequencies.
E3 — the reflection principle by Monte Carlo (target 0.556).
E4 — the Poisson-jump overlay and the tail odometer.
E1 — the W dW integral: left vs midpoint, gap T/2.
E2 — Ito's formula; classical rule fails by the QV term.
E3 — Girsanov: Q-drift equals r, volatility unchanged.
E4 — the 1/sqrt(n) hedging-error law.
E1 — the collar three ways (closed form / PDE / Monte Carlo).
E2 — the Greeks and the jump-day P&L.
E3 — reprice the collar under a volatility skew.
E4 — barrier knockout; continuous vs daily monitoring.
E1 — the tangency portfolio and the Sharpe gap.
E2 — estimation fragility and the shrinkage defense.
E3 — Merton's constant weight and implied gamma.
E4 — the Kelly parabola and the growth ceiling.
E1 — the Bellman recursion on a consumption toy.
E2 — the HJB deficit; bleed rates vs the flat optimum.
E3 — the (gamma, rho) -> nu* spending surface.
E4 — optimal execution and the shortfall frontier.
E1 — the American 90-put (1.826) and its boundary.
E2 — the call's exercise region appears as dividends rise.
E3 — smooth pasting; value maximized at S* = 66.0.
E4 — the platform: beta1, hurdle, option value, NPV.
E1 — the Gaussian update ($151.5M +/- $3.3M nowcast).
E2 — the Kalman filter and innovation whiteness.
E3 — de-smoothing: recover theta = 0.8.
E4 — learning the drift over the horizon.
E1 — the two-bond (-4, 98) VaR violation; ES coherent.
E2 — VaR and ES across confidence levels; the RU minimum.
E3 — ES over an ambiguity box.
E4 — backtesting and detection power.
E1 — reverse-engineer (gamma, beta) = (1.41, 1.026).
E2 — the Kyle auction: lambda = 0.125, market depth 8.
E3 — the margin spiral and the k -> 1 regime change.
E4 — the agenda as one problem family.
